game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **sugar, sugar:** Players interact with the game by drawing lines on the screen with a finger swipe, creating physical barriers that redirect falling sugar particles. The objective for each of the 125 levels is to guide a specific volume of sugar from a fixed source into one or more target cups, which have individual capacity requirements. As players progress, new puzzle elements are introduced, such as color filters that change sugar properties, gravitational switches that alter the direction of sugar flow, and teleports that instantly relocate particles, requiring strategic line placement. Difficulty scales by increasing the number of cups, the complexity of the environment, and the intricate interactions between these new mechanics. A level is completed when all cups receive their required sugar volume, and players advance to the next challenge.
game_info.description: Guide falling sugar particles into cups by drawing barrier lines! Each level requires you to fill all cups to their target amounts. Sugar falls from spawners and bounces off your drawn lines. Watch out for special elements: color filters change sugar colors, gravity switches flip the flow direction, and teleporters instantly transport particles. Plan your barriers carefully to direct the right amount of sugar into each cup. Complete all requirements to advance through increasingly challenging puzzles!
game_info.controls:
ARROW KEYS: Draw barrier lines (hold and drag to create barriers)
SPACE: Release a burst of sugar particles from spawners
D: Delete the most recently drawn barrier
R: Restart current level (when in GAME_OVER states)
ENTER: Start game from start screen
ESC: Pause/Unpause game

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic sugar particle spawning, physics simulation, and barrier drawing mechanics. This test verifies that particles fall correctly under gravity, barriers can be drawn and interact with particles, and the Matter.js physics integration works properly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Automatically spawn sugar particles at regular intervals. Draw several horizontal and diagonal barriers across the screen to redirect falling particles. Observe particle bouncing and collision with barriers to ensure physics behaves realistically.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Sugar particles should spawn continuously, fall under gravity, and bounce off drawn barriers with realistic physics. Barriers should remain stable and not glitch. Particles should accumulate at the bottom or in cups naturally. Test is successful when particles interact smoothly with barriers for at least 300 frames without errors.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing win condition by automatically filling cups to completion. This validates that the game correctly tracks sugar accumulation in cups, detects when all cups meet their requirements, and transitions to the win state appropriately.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Draw optimal barrier lines to guide all falling sugar directly into cups. Spawn sugar continuously at an accelerated rate. Monitor cup fill levels and ensure particles are properly detected when entering cup boundaries. Continue until all cups reach their target amounts.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: All cups should fill to their required amounts (100% for each cup). Once all requirements are met, the game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state within a reasonable timeframe (under 600 frames). The win screen should display correctly with level completion message. Test succeeds when win condition triggers properly.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing cup overflow mechanics and loss condition. This ensures that if any cup exceeds its maximum capacity, the game correctly identifies an overflow state and transitions to the loss condition.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Draw barriers that funnel excessive sugar into a single cup. Spawn sugar at an accelerated rate directed toward one cup. Monitor the cup's fill level as it approaches and exceeds its maximum capacity threshold.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: When a cup receives more sugar than its maximum capacity (110% threshold), the game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE state. The loss screen should display with an appropriate message indicating overflow. Test is successful when the overflow detection works reliably and triggers the loss condition.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing color filter mechanics. This verifies that color filters correctly change the color of sugar particles passing through them, allowing for multi-color cup requirements in later levels.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Draw barriers to direct particles through color filter zones. Spawn white/neutral sugar particles and observe color changes as they pass through red, green, or blue filters. Verify that colored particles maintain their new color after passing through.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Sugar particles should visibly change color when passing through filter zones. The color change should be immediate and persistent. Different filters should produce different colors. Test succeeds when color transitions work smoothly for at least 200 particles without visual glitches.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing gravity switch mechanics. This validates that gravity switches can flip the direction of gravity when activated, causing particles to fall upward or in different directions, adding puzzle complexity.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Ensure sugar particles pass through or near gravity switch zones. Observe the change in particle trajectory when gravity direction is modified. Test multiple gravity direction changes to ensure the mechanic works reliably.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: When particles interact with a gravity switch, the world gravity should change direction (e.g., from downward to upward). All particles should respond to the new gravity direction. The switch should toggle reliably. Test is successful when gravity changes work consistently and particles respond correctly.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing teleporter mechanics. This verifies that teleporters correctly detect particles entering their zone and instantly relocate them to the paired destination teleporter.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Draw barriers to guide particles into teleporter entrance zones. Monitor particles as they enter and should instantly appear at the exit teleporter location. Test with multiple particles to ensure consistent behavior.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Particles entering a teleporter entrance should instantly disappear and reappear at the exit teleporter with maintained velocity. No particles should be lost or duplicated. Teleportation should be visually clear with appropriate effects. Test succeeds when teleportation works reliably for at least 50 particles.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing barrier deletion and level progression. This ensures players can delete barriers to adjust their strategy and that completing a level properly advances to the next level with increased difficulty.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Draw several barriers, then systematically delete them using the delete key. Complete the current level by filling all cups, then verify that the game advances to the next level with new challenges or increased difficulty.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Barriers should be deleted one at a time (most recent first) when the delete key is pressed. After winning a level, the game should automatically load the next level with reset state and potentially new mechanics. Test is successful when barrier deletion works correctly and level progression is smooth without errors.
